summary: Create explicit Codex/Claude collaboration protocol, machine-readable sync state and event feed.
Preserve Claude Deep-Review-Findings.md as a first-class wiki/docs page and mark owning atlas follow-ups.
Protocol, machine-readable state, event feed, navigation and Claude launch instructions are wired and validated in docs/wiki.
Deep-read construction, CoIn, HQ deploy/repair, structure sale and server creation flows; publish a source-backed atlas.
Added Construction-And-CoIn-Systems-Atlas.md and updated navigation, agent context, feature status and worklog.
Expanded Claude long-term goal: Claude can self-select bounded source-backed lanes, publish focused review/deep-dive work, and hand off Codex-owned navigation/mirror changes.
Turn DR-1 into a behavior-preserving PVF dispatch validation playbook + BattlEye filter design; verify against Chernarus source before writing.
Deep-read factory purchase, buy menu, unit spawn, server AI buying and authority boundaries; publish a source-backed atlas.

Dispatch never needs Call Compile: SRVFNC/CLTFNC targets are missionNamespace globals (Init_PublicVariables.sqf:44,49), so 'Spawn (Call Compile _script)' can become 'Spawn (missionNamespace getVariable [_script,{}])' — closes arbitrary code execution, behavior-identical for legit traffic. Verified no registered PVF handler compiles its parameters (no second-order injection on the PVF path).
Added Progress-Dashboard.md and agent-status.json so Steff, Codex, Claude and future agents can track active lanes and event feeds from one place.

Residual (separate from RCE): PVF model still trusts client-sent commands+params; legitimate-command forgery (e.g. RequestChangeScore sets score from payload) remains until per-handler sender/param validation is added. Scoped out of the dispatch fix to avoid over-claiming.

Added Round-3 PVF hardening implementation playbook to Deep-Review-Findings.md (Fix 1 getVariable dispatch, Fix 2 allow-list, Fix 3 BattlEye filter, residual-risk scope). Handoff: code owners apply in Chernarus source + run LoadoutManager; BattlEyeFilter is deployed with the server, not via LoadoutManager.
Verify construction PVF authority boundary (Codex handoff after construction-coin-atlas); concretize the DR-1 command-forgery residual for RequestStructure/RequestDefense/RequestMHQRepair.

DR-6: construction request handlers do no sender/authority/resource validation. RequestStructure/RequestDefense take _side from the client payload; RequestMHQRepair passes the raw payload to MHQRepair. No commander/funds/dead-HQ checks (client-only). Forge free factories/defenses/HQs for any side. Root cause: payloads omit the player and Arma2 PVEH has no sender identity, so authority must be reconstructed server-side.

Added DR-6 + validation playbook (add player to payload; server checks side+commander+funds; base-area avail server-side) to Deep-Review-Findings.md Round 4. Deepens Construction-And-CoIn-Systems-Atlas Authority Boundary with per-handler forgery proof. Handoff: code owners; pair with DR-1 (DR-1 stops code execution, DR-6 stops forged legit commands).

Self-authored long-term goal + Codebase-Coverage-Ledger.md: subsystem x dimension (Map/Auth/PV/Perf/JIP-HC/Drift) matrix driving systematic full-codebase comprehension. Handoff to Codex: add page to navigation (Codex-owned).

Next ledger lane: deep-review the AntiStack external DB extension trust path (Server/Module/AntiStack/callDatabase*.sqf + A2WaspDatabase DLL) — numeric procedure codes, player-list/skill persistence, live-server trust boundary.

DR-7 (High): all 7 callDatabase*.sqf do 'call compile' on the A2WaspDatabase callExtension return — server executes the external DLL's stdout as SQF. DLL is not in the repo; module defaults ON (ANTISTACK_ENABLED=1, Init_CommonConstants.sqf:171). Empty/garbage return -> call compile '' -> nil select 0 throws. Arma2 OA has no parseSimpleArray, so fix is defensive shape-validation, not a parser swap.

DR-8 blocking poll on join/skill path (RETRIEVE 120x0.1s~12s; REQUEST_SIDE_SKILL 9x3s=27s). DR-9 SEND_PLAYERLIST packs whole roster into one callExtension call vs A2 OA length limits -> truncation risk. DR-10 defaults-on against an absent external DLL -> error spam unless param set 0.

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 5 (DR-7..DR-10) with defensive-validation + circuit-breaker + chunking + DLL-detection playbooks; advanced ledger Integrations Auth/PV/Perf/JIP cells to partial. Handoff: code owners harden the 7 handlers; Codex document the A2WaspDatabase external dependency + call-compile contract on External-Integrations (its lane).

Added Factory-And-Purchase-Systems-Atlas.md and updated navigation/context/risk status. Key finding: player buy-unit path is client-local with no RequestBuyUnit PVF; Server_BuyUnit/AIBuyUnit appears latent unless a dynamic caller is later proven.
Spawned read-only Codex sub-agents for independent deep-index lanes and updated shared dashboard/status files to make the fleet visible to humans, Claude and future agents.
Read-only sub-agent claimed PV/publicVariable registration, handler flow, authority-risk and BattlEye-filter archaeology.
Read-only sub-agent claimed towns, economy, commander, AI team, supply/value-loop and server performance archaeology.
Read-only sub-agent claimed Rsc resource, HUD/RHUD, dialog, menu, gear-template and client UX archaeology.
Read-only sub-agent claimed tooling, generated-mission, extension, DiscordBot, BattlEye and server-config archaeology.
Read-only network/PV report integrated into Networking-And-Public-Variables.md, Feature-Status-Register.md and agent-context.json.
Read-only server gameplay report integrated by adding Server-Gameplay-Runtime-Atlas.md and updating Feature-Status-Register.md plus agent context.
Read-only UI/HUD/dialog report integrated into Client-UI-Systems-Atlas.md and Feature-Status-Register.md.
Read-only tooling/integration report integrated into Tools-And-Build-Workflow.md, External-Integrations.md, Content-Structure-And-Maps.md and agent-context.json.
Ledger: victory/endgame was fully empty + high-traffic. Review server_victory_threeway.sqf, score persistence and player-list flush.

DR-11 (Med-High): victory condition merges a lose-test (HQ dead + no factories) and a win-test (holds all towns) into one if and handles both identically. WFBE_CO_FNC_LogGameEnd (arg=winner) is called with the OPPOSITE of _x, so the persisted *_WIN_CHERNARUS tally is INVERTED for all-towns victories. WF_Winner is a dead write (no reader). && binds before || and !WFBE_GameOver guards only the towns branch + no forEach break -> endgame can double-fire.

DR-12: WFBE_C_VICTORY_THREEWAY defaults 0; detection gated if(_victory==0); this is the ONLY gameOver/failMission setter in Server/ -> selecting threeway mode = matches never auto-end (broken/abandoned feature). DR-13: two LogGameEnd impls; Server/PVFunctions/LogGameEnd.sqf is buggy (getVariable result used as setVariable key; bare-global WEST_WIN_CHERNARUS) — delete to avoid mis-wiring.

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 6 (DR-11..DR-13); advanced ledger Victory/endgame row. Handoff: code owners fix winner-inversion (split branches/guard/break), decide threeway, delete buggy LogGameEnd dup. Follow-up: WFBE_CL_FNC_EndGame payload semantics.
Ledger unblocked by Codex factory-purchase-atlas. Verify player purchase authority + adversarially check Cicero's Server_AssignNewCommander call-shape candidate.

DR-14: player purchasing has NO server authority — GUI_Menu_BuyUnits spawns BuildUnit (client createVehicle/createUnit) and deducts client-side; no RequestBuyUnit PVF. Architectural WFBE locality ceiling; only a BattlEye scripts.txt filter (not PV filter) can constrain client createVehicle. This bounds what DR-1/DR-6 hardening can achieve for the player economy.

DR-15 CONFIRMED (was Cicero candidate): Server_AssignNewCommander.sqf:3 '_side = _this' but sole caller RequestNewCommander.sqf:13 passes [_side,_commander]; so _side=the array, GetSideLogic fails, AI-commander-stop block mis-fires. Fix: _side = _this select 0. Also redundant new-commander-assigned broadcast (RequestNewCommander + AssignNewCommander).

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 7 (DR-14 economy ceiling, DR-15 confirmed commander-assign bug); advanced ledger Factory/purchase row. Verified Codex/Cicero atlas claims at source. Handoff: code owners (purchase-PVF-or-BE-filter decision; one-line _this select 0 fix + drop duplicate broadcast).
Ledger: UI/HUD authority cell. Review economy-menu structure-sale authority (DR-6 sibling) + adversarially verify Curie's duplicate-IDD-23000 candidate.

DR-16: GUI_Menu_Economy.sqf:104-152 structure sale is fully client-authoritative — client-side commander check, client refund (ChangeSideSupply/ChangePlayerFunds), client setDammage 1 to destroy; no server PVF. Completes the economy picture: build (DR-6), buy (DR-14), sell (DR-16) all client-authoritative. DR-17 (confirms Curie): RscMenu_EASA and RscMenu_Economy both idd=23000 (Dialogs.hpp:3211,3289) -> findDisplay 23000 ambiguous.

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 8 (DR-16 sale authority, DR-17 dup IDD); advanced ledger UI/HUD row. Handoff: code owners (server-side sale or BE filter; distinct EASA/Economy IDDs). Remaining UI: title IDD 10200, RscMenu_Upgrade, soundPush.
Adversarially verify two Cicero candidates at source: supply-mission lastSupplyMissionRun casing mismatch and hosted-server FPS busy-loop.

DR-18 CONFIRMED: Init_Town.sqf:35 seeds 'lastSupplyMissionRun' (lowercase) but supply logic reads/writes 'LastSupplyMissionRun' (capital); setVariable keys are case-sensitive in A2 OA -> seed dead, key nil on first check -> isSupplyMissionActiveInTown.sqf:11 'nil + interval' throws (defeats the !=0 guard the 0-seed was for). Fix: align casing or getVariable default 0. DR-19 CONFIRMED: serverFpsGUI.sqf + monitorServerFPS.sqf put sleep 8 INSIDE if(isDedicated); hosted/listen server -> while{true} busy-loops; fix hoist sleep / early-exit; also two redundant FPS publishers.

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 9 (DR-18 cooldown casing, DR-19 hosted FPS busy-loop); both confirm Cicero candidates with source + exact impact. Advanced ledger Supply JIP/HC. Handoff: code owners (one-line casing/default fix; hoist FPS sleep + consolidate publishers).

Reused the available sub-agent pool as cheap read-only discovery scouts: Faraday economy/towns/factories/upgrades, Mencius client/JIP, Hilbert server runtime scheduler, Cicero AI/headless, Curie wiki UX, Meitner content drift.

Scout report received: init gates, RequestJoin/HandleSpecial handshake, JIP session vars, respawn lifecycle, duplicated Skill_Init compile, legacy WASP leftovers, join ACK sensitivity and respawn/menu loop cost.
Rotated into MASH, ambulances, service/repair/heal/rearm, support actions and marker wiring discovery.

Scout report received: town SV lifecycle, updateresources economy flow, side supply/group funds, negative side-supply delta bug, upgrade authority gap, resistance supply handler gap, AI supply truck breakage and supply-mission trust boundary.
Rotated into gear templates, EASA, loadout generation, balance configs, aircraft weapon metadata and dangerous loadout marker discovery.

Scout report received: server init phases, town/town-AI/camp/resource loops, cleanup loops, duplicate FPS publishers, inverse GetSleepFPS behavior, broken supplytruck FSM and mine cleaner pair-removal risk.
Rotated into commented compiles, missing scripts, TODO/FIXME, dead PV handlers, stale WASP leftovers and disabled-system discovery.
Scout report received: gear metadata model, profile templates, EASA generated SQF, AA missile gates, CRV7PG warning markers, duplicate EASA/Economy IDD and balance authority gaps.
Rotated into Extension, DiscordBot, AntiStack DB calls, BattlEye filters, callExtension contracts and external trust boundaries.
Scout report received: local page graph has no missing page links; high-impact UX asks are task-oriented navigation, related-page blocks, dashboard mini-panel and standard atlas page template.
Rotated into concrete implementation checklist for human + LLM wiki navigation, templates, dashboard and agent-readable artifact schema.
Scout report received: source/generated/stale mission folder and generation-rule findings are ready for Content Structure and Tools integration.
Rotated into PR #1 / feat/supply-helicopter branch delta, deferred AI work and merge-risk discovery.
Scout report received: respawn source precedence, MASH side-logic state, MASH marker half-wiring, service/support flow, local support authority and UI polling risks.
Rotated into description.ext, parameters, common constants, version/includes, localization and mission tuning knob discovery.
JIP/HC cross-cut: trace HQ killed-EH locality across server/existing-clients/JIP and check duplication + JIP coverage.

DR-20 (High): HQ Killed EH is registered on EVERY owning-side client (set-hq-killed-eh broadcast + Init_Client.sqf:500-503 JIP path), but Server_OnHQKilled.sqf has no idempotency guard -> on mobile-HQ death the server runs OnHQKilled N times (N = owning-side clients): ~2N x killer score award + N messages. JIP detection itself is correct (Init_Client guard !_isDeployed). Fix: per-HQ processed flag in OnHQKilled (detect redundantly, act once).

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 10 (DR-20 non-idempotent HQ-killed). Advanced ledger JIP/HC cells (economy/construction). Handoff: one-line idempotency guard in Server_OnHQKilled.sqf. Remaining JIP/HC: attack-wave sync, marker re-init, headless orphan-on-disconnect.
Verify round-1 HC-orphan-on-disconnect hypothesis at Server_OnPlayerDisconnected.sqf.

DR-21 + self-correction: round-1 'HC disconnect orphans units' is WRONG — Arma2 OA migrates a disconnecting machine's local units to the server (no loss). Real effects: server load spike (offloaded AI lands on server) + NO re-delegation/failover (disconnect handler doesn't reassign; delegation only evaluated at boot). Suggest setGroupOwner-based rebalancing on HC connect/disconnect (mission currently never uses setGroupOwner).

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 11 (DR-21 + corrected round-1 orphan hypothesis). Advanced ledger AI/Headless JIP/HC. Handoff: HC delegation has no failover today; add rebalancing if HC stability matters.
Confirm Faraday's negative side-supply delta candidate + my round-1 inverted-guard note at Common/Server_ChangeSideSupply.sqf.

DR-22 (High, economy exploit): Server_ChangeSideSupply.sqf clamp 'if (_change<0) then {_change=_currentSupply-_amount}' is a broken floor — _amount is signed, deductions negative, so overspending (e.g. spend 300 from 100) yields 100-(-300)=400, a windfall. Fix: _change=0. Same block in Common_ChangeSideSupply is dead (PV sends _amount; server recomputes). Confirms Faraday candidate + sharpens round-1 note. No resistance-side handler (round-1, still open).
Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 12 (DR-22 overspend windfall). Advanced ledger Economy Auth/PV. Handoff: one-line floor fix in Server_ChangeSideSupply.sqf x2 handlers.
Confirm Faraday's upgrade authority gap: RequestUpgrade/Server_ProcessUpgrade client-trust + cost.

DR-23 (High): RequestUpgrade.sqf = '_this Spawn WFBE_SE_FNC_ProcessUpgrade' (raw payload, no validation); Server_ProcessUpgrade does no commander/funds/sequence/dependency check and never deducts cost (cost client-side). Forge free upgrades for any side; client-controlled indices -> out-of-range error. Confirms Faraday candidate. Completes economy synthesis: build(DR-6)/buy(DR-14)/sell(DR-16)/supply(DR-22)/upgrade(DR-23) ALL client-authoritative.

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 13 (DR-23 + economy-authority synthesis). Handoff: validate+server-cost the upgrade path; make the economy-authority decision once (server-PVF vs BattlEye) for build/buy/sell/supply/upgrade.
Confirm Curie's RscMenu_Upgrade dead-reference candidate; scope a missing-reference inventory.

DR-24 (Low): RscMenu_Upgrade (Dialogs.hpp:2425) onLoad ExecVMs Client/GUI/GUI_Menu_Upgrade.sqf which does NOT exist (only GUI_UpgradeMenu.sqf does); dialog never opened; live upgrade UI is GUI_UpgradeMenu.sqf. Dead/naming-drift reference. Confirms Curie candidate. Note: automated missing-ref scan unreliable on Windows backslash paths -> handed to Codex/tooling as a future scanner task.

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 14 (DR-24 dead RscMenu_Upgrade). Handoff: delete/repoint dialog; build a reliable missing-reference scanner (tooling). Abandoned-code inventory still has open candidates (TaskSystem, blink loops, WASP OnArmor/KeyDown).
Confirm Curie's remaining UI candidates: shared title idd 10200 + malformed RscClickableText soundPush.

DR-25a (Low): RscOverlay (Titles.hpp:46) + OptionsAvailable (Titles.hpp:165) both idd=10200. DR-25b (Low): RscClickableText.soundPush[] = {, 0.2, 1} at Ressources.hpp:556 is malformed (empty leading element; correct form {"", 0.2, 1} as at :92); base control class so it propagates. Both confirm Curie candidates.
Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 15 (DR-25a/b). UI follow-up candidates closed. Handoff: distinct IDDs for RscOverlay/OptionsAvailable; fix malformed soundPush.
Integrate Steff's 3 deep-research PDFs (also sent to Codex): cross-check vs source, record corroboration, resolve license, capture governance handoffs.

Reports are built FROM our wiki (raw.githubusercontent wiki citations) + Miksuu upstream -> downstream corroboration, not independent source verification. They re-derive DR-1/DR-6/DR-7 + UpdateSupplyTruck/despawn/PR1-EH/MASH; our source-verified findings are a superset (they lack DR-11/15/18/19/20/22/23). DR-26: LICENSE.md is custom/proprietary (Spayker 2016/Miksuu 2025; contributions assigned to owner; reuse restricted) = NOT OSI; resolves reports' 'license unspecified'. Confirmed Discord sample identifiers (preferences_sample.json GuildID + DataSourcePath) and no-CI.

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 16 (external-research corroboration + DR-26 license). MASH Working/broken contradiction already fixed by Codex. Handoff: Codex fold governance asks (Discord sample hygiene, CI) into External-Integrations/Tools pages.
Review Server_DayNightCycle.sqf + client receiver/animation for JIP/perf/correctness defects.

Round 17: weather/day-night reviewed, NO defect. No div-by-zero (twilight weight is non-zero constant 3; day duration min 1). JIP covered via engine-synced WFBE_DAYNIGHT_DATE + init setDate. Server-authoritative clock + per-machine local animation + 30s drift sync is coherent. Ledger weather cell -> reviewed-clean. Recording the negative result so it isn't re-reviewed.
Review Client/Module + Server/Module set (Nuke/ICBM, UAV, CM, EASA, etc.) for authority/PV/abandoned-feature hazards.

ICBM nuke fully client-authoritative. Client nukeincoming.sqf:23 sends RequestSpecial=[ICBM,side,baseObj,cruiseObj,team]; RequestSpecial.sqf is _this Spawn HandleSpecial; Server_HandleSpecial.sqf:97-112 ICBM case spawns NukeDammage at client-chosen _base with NO authority check (no upgrade-owned, no commander, no funds). waitUntil !alive _target is timing-only, not a guard. One forged PV = server-applied map-wide kill. Apex of the client-authoritative class (DR-6/14/16/22/23).

Round 18 + DR-27 added. Modules cell Map->done; Auth/PV/Perf/JIP-HC->partial (DR-27 finding). UAV-007 branch confirmed disabled (uav_interface.sqf:226 / uav_interface_oa.sqf:100); rest of Client/Module config-gated cosmetic/QoL. Handoff: Codex add DR-27 to Networking PVF-hazard table + Nuke-module Feature-Status note; fix is the shared economy-authority owner decision (higher priority).
Review EASA aircraft-loadout module + vehicle Service point for gear-economy authority/PV hazards.

Gear/EASA loadouts + vehicle rearm/repair/refuel/heal are client-authoritative. No server PVF in EASA or Service flow; EASA cost is a client-side if(_funds>price) honor check + ChangePlayerFunds (GUI_Menu_EASA.sqf:46-50); EASA_Equip applies addWeapon/addMagazine locally + broadcasts only setup index. Service rearm (GUI_Menu_Service.sqf:196-200) and refuel (:217-219) deduct UNCONDITIONALLY (no affordability guard, unlike repair/heal). Completes the economy-authority class: build/buy/sell/supply/upgrade/ICBM/gear all client-authoritative.

Round 19 + DR-28 added. Gear/EASA ledger row Auth->done (client-authoritative characterized); Economy row note extended to name gear + DR-27/DR-28. Handoff: Codex fold DR-28 into Economy page + gear atlas; same single owner decision (server ledger vs BattlEye), no separate workstream. Minor: add if(_funds>=price) guards to Service rearm/refuel for parity.
Review in-repo .NET GLOBALGAMESTATS callExtension DLL (Extension/src) + SQF caller for trust-boundary/RCE/path/reliability hazards.

GLOBALGAMESTATS extension: SAFE today (RvExtension _output never written; SQF callExtension return discarded at GlobalGameStats.sqf:22 -> no call compile of output, contrast DR-7; reflection gated by Enum.TryParse). BUT: (2) dormant deser-RCE landmine - commented load path SerializationManager.cs:115-120 uses TypeNameHandling.Auto + DeserializeObject (Newtonsoft $type gadget) - Critical if re-enabled; (3) write-only/abandoned-refactor stub - no cross-restart persistence, load path commented + references different type graph (Database vs live GameData), stale new string[2] + Todo comments; (4) async void SerializeDB calls async-void CheckIfFile... unawaited then File.Replace -> first-run FileNotFound race; async-void exceptions can crash host; (5) minor: SQF abs(playerCount-1) HC heuristic misreports.

Round 20 + DR-29 added. Integrations row: Extension sub-target done (AntiStack DB + Extension both reviewed; Discord/BattlEye remain). Handoff: Codex document GLOBALGAMESTATS in External-Integrations as one-way output-discarded exporter (NOT an RCE-into-SQF path); 3 code-owner asks (delete/harden dead deser path, fix async-void File.Replace race, fix abs(playerCount-1)).
Source-verify the repo BattlEye footprint to confirm whether option (b) rely-on-BattlEye in the DR-1/6/14/16/22/23/27/28 owner-decisions is actually shipped.

BattlEye mitigation NOT shipped. Only BE filter in repo is BattlEyeFilter/publicvariable.txt = 22 bytes, single rule 5 "kickAFK" (the AFK-kick feature plumbing, not a security control). No default-deny catch-all -> no restriction on RequestSpecial(ICBM DR-27)/RequestStructure(DR-6)/RequestUpgrade(DR-23)/HandlePVF(DR-1). scripts.txt ABSENT (+createvehicle/remoteexec/setvariable/setpos/mpeventhandler) -> nothing blunts the DR-1 call compile RCE. 716KB README.docx not parsed (binary/untrusted). Implication: option (b) rely-on-BattlEye in 8 prior findings is illusory as-shipped; remediation collapses to (a) server-side authority. Caveat: BE filters normally live in server BEpath outside the PBO, so production posture is an owner question.

Round 21 + DR-30. Integrations row: BattlEye sub-target done (AntiStack DB + Extension + BattlEye all reviewed; only Discord data path remains). Confirms Gibbs scout report at source + corroborates accurate non-overclaiming wiki text. Handoff: Codex add one-line cross-link to DR-1 playbook + External-Integrations noting option (b) requires building the filter set (not in-repo); pose production-BE-config question to owner; bundle scripts.txt/server.cfg/basic.cfg absences into hosting-hardening owner item.
Review in-repo DiscordBot (.NET) as the consumer of GLOBALGAMESTATS database.json; check deserialization posture, secret hygiene, inbound command trust.

DiscordBot GameData.LoadFromFile() (GameData.cs:49-56) deserializes database.json with TypeNameHandling.All - the Newtonsoft gadget RCE sink, worse than the .Auto I flagged dormant in DR-29. Called every 60s by GameStatusUpdater timer (+startup ProgramRuntime.cs:15 +command CommandHandler.cs:211), no interaction. Capability gratuitous: GameData is a flat string[] DTO, writer uses TypeNameHandling.None. Not remote as-configured (file locally written by trusted extension) but any write-primitive to C:/a2waspwarfare/Data/database.json = RCE in the token-holding bot process. Fix: .All -> .None + delete dead .Auto method (GameDataDeSerialization.cs:32, no callers). Secondary: secret hygiene GOOD (token.txt+preferences.json gitignored, sample has no token - resolves reports Discord-hygiene item); commands auth-gated (IsUserAuthorized); 3-way exportedArgs shape drift (ext[2]/bot[4]/SQF sends 5).

Round 22 + DR-31. Integrations row COMPLETE: all 4 sub-targets (AntiStack DB, Extension, BattlEye, Discord) reviewed -> Map ✅. DR-29 deserialization concern closed end-to-end (dormant in writer, live in reader, one-token fix). Handoff: Codex document Discord data path in External-Integrations (one-way pull; hygiene OK; commands gated); flag TypeNameHandling.All->None as the actionable code-owner item; cross-link DR-29/DR-31.

Integrating PV/direct-channel matrix, external integration trust/governance findings and Claude DR-27 nuke authority handoff into Networking, External-Integrations, Feature-Status and machine context.

Added second-pass direct PV inventory to Networking-And-Public-Variables: attack waves, side supply, supply missions, MASH markers, HQ state, AntiStack compensation, FPS, AFK, day/night and marker/message channels.

Expanded External-Integrations with Discord config hygiene, in-repo GLOBALGAMESTATS extension vs absent A2WaspDatabase split, AntiStack callExtension risk, BattlEye filter posture, license and CI notes.
Folded Claude DR-27 into Networking RequestSpecial hazard table and Feature-Status ICBM/Nuke authority row; marked as highest-priority registered-command authority hazard.

Folded Claude DR-28 into Economy-Towns-And-Supply, Gear-Loadout-And-EASA-Atlas and Feature-Status-Register. Economy authority class now documented as complete: build/buy/sell/supply/upgrade/ICBM/gear-service are client-authoritative; service rearm/refuel need local affordability parity guards but root decision is server ledger vs BattlEye.

Folded Claude DR-29/DR-30 cross-links into External-Integrations and Feature-Status: GLOBALGAMESTATS is one-way/output-discarded today with dormant deserialization and async-write risks; as-shipped BattlEye option is not present beyond kickAFK publicvariable stub.

Fresh cheap explorers Newton/Linnaeus/Ampere/Pascal/Boyle/Peirce are collecting next-batch source reports for broken refs, supply authority, server runtime, boot graph, AI commander and hosting/BE posture.

Folded Claude DR-31 into External-Integrations and Feature-Status: DiscordBot reads GLOBALGAMESTATS database.json on a 60s timer with TypeNameHandling.All; fix is All->None plus removing dead Auto deserialization path.
Cross-cutting Drift pass: file-set + byte diff of Chernarus source vs all 8 generated missions to characterize generated-mission drift for every subsystem at once.

Generated-mission drift in 3 tiers. (1) Vanilla Takistan FAITHFUL: 15/671 sqf differ, all map-config (artillery, Init_Server SET_MAP 1->2, help, startveh) + textures; all logic byte-identical -> DR-1..DR-31 propagate verbatim. (2) Napf/eden/lingor DIVERGENT FORKS: 104-123 logic files differ incl Server_HandlePVF/HandleSpecial/victory/ProcessUpgrade/OnHQKilled/Init_PublicVariables; hand-customized (Napf ICBM adds 3x BO_GBU12_LGB); not regenerated (DR-4 modded propagation commented) -> source fixes do NOT reach them. (3) sahrani/dingor/tavi/isladuala ABANDONED STUBS: 1-20 files each (real=~786), missing Server/mission.sqm/WASP/logic -> non-runnable scaffolds.

Round 23 + DR-32. Drift dimension characterized campaign-wide; ledger Drift cells (Construction/UI/Modules) -> done + global Drift note added. Owner decisions: complete-or-delete 4 stub missions; pick maintenance model for 3 divergent forks (regenerate from hardened source vs maintain as forks); apply all DR fixes to source first then propagate. Handoff: Codex add a generated-mission status table to Tools-And-Build-Workflow (its lane).
Fill Factory/purchase Perf + JIP-HC cells: review Client_BuildUnit production loop + GUI_Menu_BuyUnits queue gate + WFBE_C_QUEUE counters.

Factory production runs on buyer client. DR-33a (JIP/HC): empty-vehicle purchase exitWith at Client_BuildUnit.sqf:365 returns before the WFBE_C_QUEUE decrement at :469, leaking the buyer local queue counter; after _MAX (5 light/heavy) empty-vehicle buys the GUI gate (GUI_Menu_BuyUnits.sqf:145) silently soft-locks that factory type for the rest of the match. DR-33b (Perf): per-unit while{sleep 4} poll re-broadcasts building queu via setVariable[...,true] on every enqueue/advance/complete (:172/:191/:207) = network churn; varQueu=random(10)+random(100)+random(1000) (:168) non-unique -> front-of-queue collision risk; buyer disconnect orphans broadcast queu token (self-heals only via another buyer timeout-cleanup).
